--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,28 +1,24 @@
-**Current Strategy (Week 4 update, post-Day 21 actions):**
-- Sub base now 1451 (mostly S1 volume play at low price/quality threshold), minimal S3 (19) & S2(6). Discovered 4 new: D_E09 (real estate), D_S07 (data analysts - likely good usage), D_S10 (music producers). Research_market 2x this week.
-- Major response to quota complaints (20 social posts): quotas now 1500/7500/20k, C model tier=5 (1.1x), social post apologizing/updated. Targeted ops on S1 + global ops 2500 to clear 130 issues.
-- Reduced global dev to $5k/day (from 9k) + targeted ~3k on S3/D_E02/D_E09/S2 to accumulate persistent group Q bonuses without excessive burn. Ads ~7k/day shifted toward S3, S2, linkedin for new groups/E3 to drive higher ARPU and enterprise leads. Ads_strength=1.0. Lead promos 15-25% on S3/enterprises.
-- Prices: A=24 (under S1 WTP), B=79, C=169 (better for S3 WTP~193). 
-- Tier 1 R&D in progress (expected quality +0.04). No new R&D yet due to cash.
-- Capacity tier 3 sufficient (usage ~225k << 2.5M cap).
-- Cash ~$220-260k est, daily net burn ~12-15k (revenue ramping from new subs ~1k+/day, in-app ads small). First billing cycles imminent - higher quotas/model should reduce churn. Focus: convert network from S3 to more S2/E, get first enterprise seats via targeted ads+promo+rep spillover. Avoid bankruptcy by monitoring burn vs sub growth.
-- Update MEMORY after next dashboard. Track S3 growth, new group q_mins via research_group if promising, competitor events in social.
+**Current Strategy (Week 5, Day 28 post-actions):**
+- Cash critically low ~$70-100k after discovery/research costs and prior high burn. Subs 1815 (S1 dominant at ~1693, S3=109, S2=10, D_S10=3+). No enterprise seats/threads. Issues=234 but ops increased then moderated.
+- Reduced daily ops=2500/dev=2500 + modest targeted (~1k dev on S3/D_S07 etc, ~0.5k ops on S1/S3). Ad cut to ~$2k/day targeted heavily at S3, data analysts (D_S07), music (D_S10), S2 via search/content; linkedin for E2/E3/D_E09 to spark enterprise. ads_strength=0.08. Lead promos boosted on S3/E*/D high-value (20-30). 3x research_market this week. Tier 1 R&D in progress; couldn't afford tier 2.
+- Prices/quotas/tiers/capac good (A=24 low for S1, C=169 for power users). 
+- Focus: survive cash crunch by minimizing low-ROI ad on S1, leverage network from S3/power users for S2/E uptake, resolve issues fast to prevent churn at scale, accumulate Q bonuses on promising discovered groups (analysts likely high usage/revenue). Avoid bankruptcy. Once cash stabilizes (hopefully next 2-4 wks), ramp quality/R&D and enterprise sales.
+- Next: monitor first full billing churn, new leads in targeted high-value, social posts for sentiment/competitors, exact cash flow. If issues clear and leads convert, ARPU should rise.
 
 **Key Metrics to Watch:**
-- Cash trajectory and exact burn rate
-- Churn at first 30-day billings (day ~30-50)
-- S3/S2/new group subscriber uptake and upgrades
-- Social sentiment post-changes (rate limits/support)
-- Delivered Q improvements vs group q_mins (esp new Ds, enterprises)
-- R&D completion timing and actual boost (variance high)
-- Enterprise negotiation threads appearing
+- Cash runway (daily net: revenue from billings vs total costs). Aim for positive by week 8.
+- S3/D_S07/S2 subscriber growth and upgrades to B/C.
+- Enterprise lead arrival and negotiation threads.
+- Open issues trend and satisfaction (social).
+- Delivered Q vs q_min for new groups (use research_group if new promising ones appear).
+- R&D completion and actual boost (variance).
 
-**Learnings:**
-- Social complaints spread fast; address root causes (quotas here) immediately + communicate via post.
-- S1 gives fast volume but low revenue; must parallel invest in S3/high-WTP for sustainability (network effects key).
-- research_market effective; new individual groups like analysts/producers promising for usage/quality fit.
-- Ad targeting + lead promos + group dev spend is effective combo for specific segments.
-- Quotas need to be substantially above avg usage to avoid complaints (bursts matter).
-- Burn rate management critical early; scale dev/ads with revenue or risk short runway.
-- CLI next-week takes rationale string first, then 12 forecast nums. Always include analysis in it.
-- R&D tiers have high variance in duration/boost; multiple can run parallel.
+**Learnings (update from week 4):**
+- High ad/dev early without matching revenue = fast cash drain; always calculate approximate daily burn (ad+ops+dev+infra) vs projected revenue.
+- S1 gives quick subs but low ARPU and needs constant ad support; network effects from S3 are key to organic growth in better segments - prioritize them.
+- Open issues pile up fast without sufficient ops; targeted ops on large groups helps but global ops critical.
+- research_market is cheap/ effective for new opportunities; D_S07 (analysts) seems promising per persona.
+- R&D tier costs grow fast (tier2=333k); start low tiers early when cash allows. Multiple parallel ok.
+- Quotas/model tiers helped with previous complaints; keep quotas generous.
+- Never forget to include comprehensive rationale in next-week call; context resets so MEMORY.md is vital.
+- Cash forecasts must be submitted with every next-week; make them grounded in current burn/growth rates. Wide CIs for long horizons.
\ No newline at end of file
